meaning: n.计算,帐单,算帐,结帐,清算,估计
examples: The mechanical clock kept equal hours, and this implied a new time reckoning.
By my reckoning we were seven or eight miles from the campground.
Dividend Payment Downtrend by Addison Wiggin originally appeared in the Daily Reckoning.
Some sort of reckoning was thus likely, but it was made worse by recent mistakes.
